nativeRange.habitat: Trade cultivar collected from Vietnam. Lineage variously attributed to Rotala rotundifolia, Rotala wallichii, or considered an undescribed Rotala from the rotundifolia complex.

decorPreferences: Nutrient-rich substrate (aquasoil) plus iron-rich root tabs
HIGH light (PAR 60+) for intense red top growth
CO2 injection essentially required for tight bushy form and color
Soft acidic water deepens the red coloration
Trim hedge-style to maintain a dense colored carpet of tops

summary: A Vietnamese-collected trade cultivar of Rotala prized for its intense burgundy-to-blood-red top growth under high-tech conditions. Lineage is debated (rotundifolia complex vs wallichii vs an undescribed species). A signature 'red Dutch-style' background stem with iron-sensitive coloration.
careNotes:
Rotala 'H'Ra' is a Vietnamese trade cultivar widely used in Dutch-style and competition aquascapes for its deep burgundy-red top growth. Lineage is unsettled: some sources position it as a cultivar of Rotala rotundifolia (parent entry fw-plant-046), others align it with Rotala wallichii (entry fw-plant-163 / 'Rotala Wallichii'), and a few treat it as an undescribed Rotala from the rotundifolia complex. Functionally, the cultivar combines the leaf width of rotundifolia with a more committed red color habit. 

Care is more demanding than common R. rotundifolia: it needs HIGH light (PAR 60+), reliable CO2 injection, iron-rich macro+micro dosing, soft acidic water, and regular trimming. Under low light or low CO2, color reverts to olive-green and the cultivar loses its distinguishing feature. Hedge-trim regularly to keep a tight carpet of red tops near the back glass. Susceptible to algae if conditions slip. Cross-references: fw-plant-046 (R. rotundifolia), fw-plant-160 ('Colorata'), fw-plant-162 (R. macrandra). 

NOTE: This entry was written from genus/parent baselines after the original research dispatch hung on upstream sources. Reviewer should verify lineage and parameter specifics against Tropica or 2HR Aquarist during the manifest URL cleanup pass.

primary.name: Rotala rotundifolia parent entry (fw-plant-046)
primary.notes: Genus-baseline inheritance from researched rotala-rotundifolia parent. Dispatch attempt hung on upstream retailer sources; manifest URL cleanup pending.
